---
title: "LIS"
subtitle: "Library of Iterative Solvers for linear systems"
---

[LIS](https://www.ssisc.org/lis/) (Library of Iterative Solvers) is a parallel
C/Fortran library for solving large, sparse systems of linear equations and
eigenvalue problems with iterative methods (a wide range of Krylov-subspace
solvers and preconditioners, including algebraic multigrid).

**Version vendored:** 2.1.11 (`lis/lis-2.1.11/`).

## Role in fesm-utils

LIS provides the sparse iterative linear solvers used by downstream Earth system
models. It is built with the Fortran 90 interface and the SA-AMG (smoothed
aggregation algebraic multigrid) preconditioner enabled.

## Building

LIS is built by its own autotools through the root `Makefile`:

```bash
make lis openmp=0        # serial -> lis/lis-serial
make lis openmp=1        # OpenMP -> lis/lis-omp
```

The target runs, in `lis/lis-2.1.11/`:

```bash
./configure --prefix=<repo>/lis/lis-<variant> --enable-f90 --enable-saamg [--enable-omp]
make && make install
```

The serial and OpenMP variants install side by side into `lis/lis-serial` and
`lis/lis-omp`. Machine/compiler specifics (compiler pick, C-standard pins, Intel
runtime-library fixes) live in
[`config/libs.mk`](https://github.com/fesmc/fesm-utils/blob/main/config/libs.mk);
the vendored 2.1.11 autotools were modernized to build cleanly with recent
`icx`/`ifx`.

## Using it downstream

Reference the install prefix directly:

```
fesm-utils/lis/lis-serial/include     # lis.h, lisf.h, the lis Fortran module
fesm-utils/lis/lis-serial/lib         # liblis.a
```

## Updating the vendored source

To try a different upstream version, unpack it into the `lis/` container folder
and rebuild:

```bash
wget https://www.ssisc.org/lis/dl/lis-2.1.11.zip
unzip lis-2.1.11.zip -d lis/
rm lis-2.1.11.zip
make lis openmp=0
```
